lists.title: 10 Best Parks in ITP Atlanta
lists.seoTitle: 10 Best Parks in ITP Atlanta | atlanta news & talk
lists.seoDescription: The best parks and green spaces inside the perimeter in Atlanta, from Piedmont Park to lesser-known gems. A local's guide to getting outside ITP.
lists.intro:
Atlanta is one of the most heavily forested cities in the country, and the parks here reflect that. You can walk through old-growth canopy minutes from downtown, find hidden creeks in neighborhoods most visitors never see, and actually enjoy being outside for a solid nine months of the year.

This list covers the best parks ITP (Inside The Perimeter, for the uninitiated). These are places locals actually go, not just the ones that show up on tourist brochures. Whether you want a trail run, a picnic spot, or somewhere to let the kids burn off energy, there's a park on this list for you.

items.name: Piedmont Park
items.description:
The crown jewel of Atlanta parks. **200 acres of green space** right in Midtown with skyline views that never get old.

- **Active Oval** for pickup soccer and frisbee
- **Lake Clara Meer** for a scenic loop walk
- **Saturday Green Market** for local produce and vendors

Connects directly to the [BeltLine Eastside Trail](https://beltline.org/), so you can walk or bike over from multiple neighborhoods. *On a clear day, the Midtown skyline reflected in the lake is genuinely postcard-worthy.*
items.tip: The northern end near The Meadow is way less crowded than the southern entrance. Park on Monroe Drive and enter from there.

items.name: Grant Park
items.description:
One of Atlanta's oldest parks, right next to [Zoo Atlanta](https://zooatlanta.org/). **131 acres** with great walking paths, a historic gateway, and excellent people watching.

- **Sunday farmers market** is a neighborhood staple
- **Loop trail** is about 1.5 miles, mostly shaded
- Great **playground** and open fields for kids

*The southeast corner near the zoo entrance has the best mature tree canopy in the whole park.*
items.tip: The loop trail around the park is about 1.5 miles and mostly shaded. Great for an easy morning jog.

items.name: Freedom Park
items.description:
A **linear park** that connects the [Carter Center](https://www.cartercenter.org/) to the BeltLine, cutting through Poncey-Highland, Candler Park, and Little Five Points.

- Paved paths perfect for **biking, running, or walking**
- Beautiful mature trees near the Moreland Avenue section
- Connects to multiple neighborhoods without touching a car road

*One of the most underrated ways to cross the east side of the city entirely on greenway.*
items.tip: Use the Freedom Park path to bike from L5P all the way to Piedmont Park without touching a single car road.

items.name: Historic Fourth Ward Park
items.description:
A relatively new park (opened 2011) that turned a flood-prone area into **one of the best spots in town**. Sits right on the Eastside Trail.

- **Splash pad** for kids in summer
- **Amphitheater** with occasional events and live music
- **Stormwater pond** that doubles as a scenic lake with a loop trail

> Grab a coffee from Dancing Goats at Ponce City Market next door and walk the loop. Hard to beat on a Saturday morning.
items.tip: Grab a coffee from Dancing Goats next door at Ponce City Market and walk the loop trail. Hard to beat on a Saturday morning.

items.name: Westside Park
items.description:
Atlanta's newest major park and **the largest greenspace in the city at 280 acres**. Built on the site of the old [Bellwood Quarry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bellwood_Quarry), the views of the reservoir from the overlook are genuinely stunning.

- **Reservoir overlook** with an unreal shade of blue-green water
- Multi-use trails still being expanded
- *Still feels like a secret to many Atlantans*

This park is going to be a major destination once all the planned phases are finished.
items.tip: Head straight to the reservoir overlook when you arrive. The quarry water is an unreal shade of blue-green.

items.name: Candler Park
items.description:
A beloved neighborhood park with something for everyone. **The disc golf course** here is one of the most popular in the city.

- Pool, tennis courts, golf course, playground
- Annual **Candler Park Fall Fest** brings out the whole neighborhood
- Great community feel year-round

*Disc golf is free to play. Bring your own discs or borrow from a friendly local on the first tee.*
items.tip: The disc golf course is free to play. Bring your own discs or borrow from a friendly local on the first tee.

items.name: Olmsted Linear Park
items.description:
A chain of connected parks designed by the **[Olmsted Brothers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Olmsted_Brothers) firm** (the same family behind Central Park). Runs through Druid Hills along Ponce de Leon Avenue.

- Gorgeous old trees and open meadows
- **Very little foot traffic** compared to bigger parks
- Walk the full length from Virgilee Park to Deepdene Park for a peaceful two-mile stroll

*Some of Atlanta's prettiest scenery, and you might have it mostly to yourself.*
items.tip: Walk the full length from Virgilee Park to Deepdene Park for a peaceful two-mile stroll through some of Atlanta's prettiest scenery.

items.name: Fernbank Forest
items.description:
**65 acres of old-growth forest** right in the middle of the city. Seriously. Some of these trees are over 200 years old.

- Access through [Fernbank Museum](https://www.fernbankmuseum.org/) (with admission) or the **free WildWoods paths**
- Paved loop trail that's great for kids
- Museum trail goes deeper into the old growth

*It feels like you've been transported to the north Georgia mountains, except you're ten minutes from downtown.*
items.tip: The WildWoods outdoor experience is free and has a paved loop trail that's great for kids. The museum trail requires admission but is worth it.

items.name: Brownwood Park
items.description:
A small, well-loved park in East Atlanta. **The kind of neighborhood park where everyone knows each other's dogs.**

- Playground, tennis courts, community garden
- Mature tree canopy provides solid shade even in August
- Community garden plots sometimes have openings

*Check with the [East Atlanta Community Association](https://www.eaca.net/) if you're interested in a garden plot.*
items.tip: The community garden plots sometimes have openings. Check with the East Atlanta Community Association if you're interested.

items.name: Coan Park
items.description:
Located in the East Lake neighborhood, Coan Park has a **recently renovated playground**, walking paths, and a rec center.

- Never crowded, even on weekends
- **Rolling terrain** makes for a decent workout
- Walking loop has real hills (unlike the flat BeltLine)

*If you want a tougher walk than the BeltLine without leaving the city, this is your spot.*
items.tip: The walking loop has some real hills. If you want a tougher walk than the flat BeltLine, this is your spot.

lists.imageAlt: Midtown Atlanta skyline reflected in Lake Clara Meer at Piedmont Park
lists.imageCredit: Photo: Daniel Mayer / Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA 3.0)

lists.title: Best Coffee Shops Along the BeltLine
lists.seoTitle: Best Coffee Shops Along the Atlanta BeltLine | atlanta news & talk
lists.seoDescription: The best coffee shops on or near the Atlanta BeltLine trail. Real spots locals hit for espresso, cold brew, and work-from-cafe vibes.
lists.intro:
The BeltLine is a 22-mile loop of trails, parks, and transit that's reshaping Atlanta. It's also lined with some of the best coffee in the city. Whether you're mid-walk and need a caffeine stop or looking for a solid work-from-cafe spot with BeltLine access, these are the places to know.

This list focuses on shops that are either directly on the trail or a very short walk from it. All of them are worth a dedicated trip, not just a convenience stop.

items.name: Dancing Goats Coffee Bar (Ponce City Market)
items.description:
[Batdorf & Bronson's](https://www.batdorfcoffee.com/) flagship Atlanta location, right at the central hub of the Eastside Trail. **The espresso is consistently excellent.**

- Beautiful space with a **patio overlooking the BeltLine**
- Outstanding cold brew
- Skip the line inside and order at the outdoor window when it's open

*You can watch the whole parade of joggers, dogs, and strollers while you sip. Peak people-watching territory.*
items.tip: The cold brew here is outstanding. Skip the line inside and order at the outdoor window when it's open.

items.name: Revelator Coffee (Westside Trail)
items.description:
A Birmingham-born roaster with a **sleek outpost on the Westside Trail** near [Lee + White](https://leeandwhite.com/).

- **Pour-overs are precise** and well-executed
- Cool industrial feel to the space
- Great place to post up with a laptop

> Try the seasonal single-origin pour-over. The baristas here actually know their stuff and can guide you through the menu.
items.tip: Try the seasonal single-origin pour-over. The baristas here actually know their stuff and can guide you.

items.name: Chrome Yellow Trading Co.
items.description:
A coffee shop and general store on Krog Street, **steps from the Eastside Trail**. The vibe is part neighborhood hangout, part design store.

- **Drip coffee** is simple and good
- Stocks interesting local goods you won't find elsewhere
- Quick walk to the [Krog Street Tunnel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krog_Street_Tunnel) for street art

*Half the fun is browsing the curated shelf of local zines, ceramics, and oddities.*
items.tip: Grab a coffee and walk over to the Krog Street Tunnel to see the latest street art. It changes constantly.

items.name: Muchacho
items.description:
A **tiny takeaway window** in Summerhill that punches way above its weight. Quick detour from the Southside Trail section of the BeltLine.

- **The cortado** is one of the best in Atlanta
- **Breakfast tacos** are legit (get them with your coffee, you won't regret it)
- Cash and card accepted, line moves fast

*This is the kind of place that's impossible to walk past once you know about it.*
items.tip: Cash and card accepted. The line moves fast. Get the breakfast tacos with your coffee, you won't regret it.

items.name: Brash Coffee
items.description:
Located in [Ponce City Market](https://poncecitymarket.com/), Brash is **laser-focused on espresso**. The menu is short by design.

- Espresso, macchiato, cortado, latte. That's it.
- **Every single one is dialed in**
- Frequently features rotating guest roasters

*If you care about the craft and don't need a 30-item menu, this is your spot.*
items.tip: Ask for the guest espresso if they have one running. Brash frequently features rotating roasters.

items.name: Hodgepodge Coffeehouse
items.description:
A cozy, slightly funky neighborhood spot in East Atlanta Village with a **solid patio**. *A short ride from the planned Southeast Trail.*

- Relaxed vibe, good coffee
- **Thai iced tea** is worth trying if you want something different
- Patio is dog-friendly and shaded

Great for a low-key afternoon when you don't want the BeltLine crowds.
items.tip: Try the Thai iced tea if you want something different. The patio is dog-friendly and shaded.

items.name: Docent Coffee
items.description:
[Docent](https://docentcoffee.com/) has locations near BeltLine-adjacent neighborhoods, including one in the **Ford Factory Lofts in Poncey-Highland**.

- Excellent specialty coffee at both spots
- Ford Factory location is a **quick walk to the Eastside Trail**
- Limited seating but a great window counter for people watching on North Avenue

*The baristas here take their craft seriously without being pretentious about it.*
items.tip: The Ford Factory location has limited seating but a great window counter for people watching on North Avenue.

items.name: Little Tart Bakeshop
items.description:
Located in [Krog Street Market](https://krogstreetmarket.com/), just off the BeltLine. Not purely a coffee shop, but the **espresso is serious** and the pastries are some of the best in Atlanta.

- **The almond croissant** is famous for a reason
- Excellent seasonal pastry rotation
- Proper espresso drinks

> Get there before 10am on weekends or the almond croissants will be gone. Not an exaggeration.
items.tip: Get there before 10am on weekends or the almond croissants will be gone. Not an exaggeration.

items.name: East Pole Coffee Co.
items.description:
A roaster and cafe in the Armour Yards area near the **Eastside Trail's northern section**. One of the best work-from-cafe setups in Atlanta.

- Beans **roasted on site**
- Open, airy space with plenty of room to spread out
- Their house blend is a great everyday coffee to take home

*If you need to get actual work done while caffeinated, this is the move.*
items.tip: Buy a bag of their house blend to take home. It's a great everyday coffee and roasted fresh.

items.name: Taproom Coffee
items.description:
A **Kirkwood favorite** close to the planned eastward BeltLine extension. [Taproom](https://taproomcoffee.com/) is all about community.

- Hosts events, supports local artists
- Coffee is consistently solid
- **Generous outdoor space**

*Check their Instagram for pop-up events. They regularly host local vendors and live music.*
items.tip: Check their Instagram for pop-up events. They regularly host local vendors and live music.

lists.title: Top Restaurants in Old Fourth Ward
lists.seoTitle: Top Restaurants in Old Fourth Ward Atlanta | atlanta news & talk
lists.seoDescription: The best restaurants in Old Fourth Ward (O4W), Atlanta. From Ponce City Market favorites to neighborhood gems, these are the spots locals love.
lists.intro:
Old Fourth Ward has gone through one of the most dramatic transformations of any Atlanta neighborhood in the last decade. The BeltLine Eastside Trail runs right through it, Ponce City Market anchors the north end, and new restaurants seem to open every month. But the best spots here aren't all new. Some have been around for years, surviving and thriving through all the changes.

This list covers the restaurants worth your time in O4W, from the heavy hitters to the under-the-radar spots that deserve more attention.

items.name: Staplehouse
items.description:
One of Atlanta's most celebrated restaurants. **The tasting menu changes regularly** and features creative dishes built from seasonal Southern ingredients.

- *Intimate space, warm service without being fussy*
- Supports the [Giving Kitchen](https://thegivingkitchen.org/) nonprofit, so your meal goes toward a good cause
- Reservations are essential

> Check their site on Monday mornings when new reservation dates drop. They go fast.
items.tip: Reservations are essential and can be tough to get. Check their site on Monday mornings when new dates drop.

items.name: Tabla
items.description:
**Indian cuisine with a modern approach**, located right on the Eastside Trail. A great date night spot.

- **The dosas** are excellent
- Thoughtful cocktail menu
- BeltLine-facing patio is hard to beat for people watching

*Sit on the patio during sunset and order the lamb rogan josh. One of the best entrees on the menu.*
items.tip: Sit on the BeltLine-side patio during sunset. The lamb rogan josh is one of the best entrees on the menu.

items.name: Barcelona Wine Bar
items.description:
Spanish tapas and an **extensive wine list** in a lively, warm space. *It gets loud in the best way.*

- **Patatas bravas** are a must-order
- Well-curated cheese boards
- Weekend brunch is excellent

> The happy hour specials on wine and select tapas are a genuine deal. Go early on a weeknight.
items.tip: The happy hour specials on wine and select tapas are a genuine deal. Go early on a weeknight.

items.name: Ponce City Market Food Hall
items.description:
Not a single restaurant but a **collection of excellent stalls** under one roof in the old [Sears building](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ponce_City_Market). A food hall done right.

- **H&F Burger** (from the Holeman & Finch team)
- **Minero** (Sean Brock's taco spot)
- **Ton Ton** (ramen and dumplings)
- **Honeysuckle Gelato**

*Skip the crowds on the main level and head upstairs to The Roof for skyline views of Midtown.*
items.tip: Skip the crowds on the main level and head upstairs to the roof. The views of Midtown from The Roof at PCM are worth the elevator ride.

items.name: Vortex Bar & Grill
items.description:
A Midtown/L5P institution. The original location at 438 Moreland is the one with **the giant skull entrance**. *An experience as much as a meal.*

- Burgers are massive and unapologetic
- Atmosphere is dive-bar-meets-burger-joint
- **Strict no-kids policy**

> Get the Coronary Bypass burger if you're feeling brave. Multiple patties, fried eggs, all the fixings.
items.tip: Get the Coronary Bypass burger if you're feeling brave. It's multiple patties with fried eggs and all the fixings.

items.name: BoccaLupo
items.description:
**Handmade pasta** in a cozy, unpretentious space on North Highland. The kind of place that makes you wonder why you ever eat pasta from a box.

- **Corzetti with pork sugo** is a standout
- Menu changes often based on what's available
- Sit at the bar and watch the pasta being made

*The bartenders are great conversationalists, and watching the pasta process is mesmerizing.*
items.tip: Sit at the bar and watch the pasta being made. It's mesmerizing and the bartenders are great conversationalists.

items.name: Wrecking Bar Brewpub
items.description:
A brewpub set in the **basement of a restored Victorian mansion**. The beer is brewed on-site and the food goes way beyond typical pub fare.

- **Smoked wings** and the **burger** are both excellent
- Creative seasonal beers on tap
- The space itself is worth the visit

*Try whatever seasonal beer is on tap. The brewers here are creative and the limited releases sell out fast.*
items.tip: Try whatever seasonal beer is on tap. The brewers here are creative and the limited releases sell out fast.

items.name: Ammazza
items.description:
**Neapolitan-style pizza** cooked in a massive wood-fired oven. The dough is made with imported Italian flour and **fermented for 72 hours**.

- **Margherita** is perfect in its simplicity
- Burrata appetizer is rich enough to split
- No reservations taken

*They get packed. Arrive before 6pm on weeknights to avoid the wait.*
items.tip: They don't take reservations and it gets packed. Arrive before 6pm on weeknights to avoid the wait.

items.name: Poor Hendrix
items.description:
A **cocktail-forward restaurant** with a seasonal menu that changes frequently. *The space is intimate and dimly lit in that good-date way.*

- Small plates are inventive without being gimmicky
- **Drink program** is one of the best in the neighborhood
- The bartender's choice cocktail is always a good call

> Tell them what you like and they'll make something custom. They're good at it.
items.tip: The bartender's choice cocktail is always a good call. Tell them what you like and they'll make something custom.

items.name: Krog Street Market
items.description:
A food hall in a **converted warehouse** with a BeltLine entrance. More focused than PCM and less touristy.

- **[Gu's Dumplings](https://www.gusdumplings.com/)** (legendary Chinese dumplings)
- **Fred's Meat & Bread** (incredible sandwiches)
- **Jeni's Splendid Ice Creams**

> Gu's always has a line, and it's always worth the wait. Get the spicy wontons and pan-fried pork dumplings.
items.tip: Gu's Dumplings always has a line, and it's always worth the wait. Get the spicy wontons and pan-fried pork dumplings.

lists.title: Best Brunch Spots in Decatur
lists.seoTitle: Best Brunch Spots in Decatur GA | atlanta news & talk
lists.seoDescription: The best brunch restaurants in Decatur, Georgia. From classic Southern breakfast to creative weekend menus, these are the spots Decatur locals love.
lists.intro:
Decatur punches way above its weight when it comes to food, and brunch is no exception. This walkable little city just east of Atlanta has a restaurant scene that rivals neighborhoods three times its size. The square and the surrounding blocks are packed with places that take weekend brunch seriously.

Whether you want biscuits and gravy, a creative eggs Benedict, or a boozy Bloody Mary on a patio, Decatur has you covered. These are the spots that locals actually go to, not just the ones that photograph well.

items.name: Revival
items.description:
**Kevin Gillespie's Southern restaurant** on the Decatur square does a brunch that's hard to top. The patio overlooking the square is prime real estate.

- **Fried chicken biscuit** is legendary
- **Shrimp and grits** are textbook
- Pastry case will test your willpower

> Go early. By 10:30am on Saturdays there's usually a wait. The pastry basket is worth adding to any order.
items.tip: Go early. By 10:30am on Saturdays there's usually a wait. The pastry basket is worth adding to any order.

items.name: Brick Store Pub
items.description:
Known primarily as a **beer bar**, but the Brick Store's brunch is an underrated gem. A [Decatur institution](https://www.brickstorepub.com/) since 1997.

- Small, focused menu
- Excellent **bloody mary**
- Belgian-style space upstairs feels like a proper European pub breakfast

*Head upstairs to the Belgian bar for a quieter experience. Pair your brunch with one of their curated Belgian ales.*
items.tip: Head upstairs to the Belgian bar for a quieter experience. Pair your brunch with one of their curated Belgian ales.

items.name: Sun in My Belly
items.description:
A Kirkwood gem that's technically just outside Decatur proper but close enough to count. **The garden patio** is one of the most pleasant brunch settings in metro Atlanta.

- Menu uses lots of **local produce**
- Baked goods are made in-house
- **French toast with seasonal fruit compote** is a regular favorite

*Call ahead for weekend reservations. This place fills up for good reason.*
items.tip: The French toast with seasonal fruit compote is a regular favorite. Call ahead for weekend reservations.

items.name: White Bull
items.description:
**Italian-leaning brunch** in a charming space on Church Street. A refined brunch spot that doesn't take itself too seriously.

- **Frittata** is always well-executed
- Creative brunch cocktail menu
- House-made focaccia is excellent

*Ask if they have any fresh pasta specials running at brunch. Worth the detour.*
items.tip: The house-made focaccia is excellent. Ask if they have any fresh pasta specials running at brunch.

items.name: The Iberian Pig
items.description:
**Spanish tapas-style brunch** with a killer cocktail menu. The space is cozy and energetic.

- **Churros with chocolate** are a must
- Savory egg dishes have a Spanish twist that sets them apart
- Bottomless sangria brunch deal is one of the best values in Decatur

*Make a reservation. This place gets packed on weekends, and for good reason.*
items.tip: The bottomless sangria brunch deal is one of the best values in Decatur. Make a reservation.

items.name: Cafe Alsace
items.description:
An **Alsatian-inspired cafe** on West Ponce de Leon with excellent pastries, savory tarts, and proper French coffee.

- **Quiche** is consistently perfect
- Relaxed outdoor seating area (dog-friendly)
- Try the **tarte flambee** (Alsatian flatbread)

*Different from anything else you'll find at brunch in Decatur. A genuine European cafe experience.*
items.tip: The tarte flambee (Alsatian flatbread) is different from anything else you'll find at brunch in Decatur.

items.name: Community Q BBQ
items.description:
A barbecue joint doing brunch might sound unusual, but **Community Q makes it work**. Not fancy, but the quality speaks for itself.

- **Brisket hash with eggs** is the move
- Smoked meat omelets
- Some of the **best biscuits** in Decatur

*Don't overthink your order. Get the brisket hash.*
items.tip: The brisket hash with eggs is the move. Don't overthink it.

items.name: Saba Italian Bar + Kitchen
items.description:
A newer Italian spot on the Decatur square with a weekend brunch that's **gaining a following**.

- **Lemon ricotta pancakes** are a standout
- Espresso program is legit (proper pulls, not an afterthought)
- Solid addition to the Decatur brunch scene

*Sit at the bar for a more relaxed vibe and faster service.*
items.tip: Sit at the bar for a more relaxed vibe and faster service. The baristas pull proper espresso.

lists.title: Best Bars in East Atlanta Village
lists.seoTitle: Best Bars in East Atlanta Village (EAV) | atlanta news & talk
lists.seoDescription: The best bars in East Atlanta Village, from dive bars to craft cocktail spots. A local's guide to drinking in EAV.
lists.intro:
East Atlanta Village is one of the last truly gritty, character-filled bar districts in ITP Atlanta. While other neighborhoods have gone full-polished, EAV holds onto its rough edges, and that's exactly what makes it great. The bars here range from legendary dives to solid craft cocktail spots, and the whole strip is walkable.

If you want a night out that doesn't feel like a corporate restaurant group planned it, EAV is your move. Here are the bars worth hitting.

items.name: The EARL
items.description:
The anchor of EAV's bar and music scene. **The EARL has been hosting indie bands, punk shows, and everything in between for over two decades**, and the room still sounds great.

- The **burgers** are surprisingly good for a music venue
- The patio is a classic hang between sets
- Live music calendar is *always* worth checking

> Local move: some of the best shows in Atlanta happen here on random weeknights for under $15.
items.tip: Check the live music schedule on their website. Some of the best shows in Atlanta happen here on random Tuesday nights.

items.name: Argosy
items.description:
A gastropub with a **deep beer list**, solid cocktails, and elevated bar food. The space is large with a great patio, and it works equally well for a date or a big group hangout.

- **The burger** and the **Nashville hot chicken sandwich** are both excellent
- Back patio has *fire pits* that are perfect on cooler nights
- Beer selection leans local and rotates frequently
items.tip: The back patio has fire pits that are perfect on cooler nights. Arrive early on weekends to snag a spot.

items.name: Gaja Korean Bar
items.description:
Korean-inspired cocktails and bar snacks in a stylish space that adds real variety to the EAV strip. *The vibe is cool without trying too hard.*

- **Soju cocktails** are fun and dangerously easy to drink
- The **Korean fried chicken** is outstanding (get the gochujang sauce)
- Late-night menu is a lifesaver after a night of bar hopping
items.tip: The Korean fried chicken wings are a must. Order them with the gochujang sauce.

items.name: Flatiron
items.description:
A cocktail bar and restaurant in the **historic [Flatiron building](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flat_Iron_Building_(Atlanta))** at the center of the village. The drink menu is creative and seasonal, the food is solid New American.

- **Rooftop patio** has great views of the EAV intersection
- Cocktail menu changes with the seasons
- *One of the more polished spots on the strip, but it still fits the neighborhood*
items.tip: Head to the rooftop for sunset. It's one of the better rooftop views in east Atlanta.

items.name: Mary's
items.description:
An **LGBTQ+ bar and dance spot** that's been a cornerstone of EAV for years. The energy is always high, the DJs know what they're doing, and it's one of the most welcoming bars in the city.

- **Drag shows** are legendary (get there early for a good spot)
- DJs keep the dance floor moving all night
- *The kind of place where strangers become friends by last call*
items.tip: The drag shows on weekends are not to be missed. Get there early for a good spot.

items.name: The Midway Pub
items.description:
A **proper dive bar** with cheap beer, pool tables, and zero pretense. *This is where you go when you want a $4 PBR and a game of pool without any fuss.*

- **Jukebox** is solid and the regulars are friendly
- Pool tables are first come, first served
- Cash is king here (there's an ATM inside)
items.tip: Cash is king here. There's an ATM inside but bring your own to avoid the fee.

items.name: Banshee
items.description:
**Craft cocktails and a curated wine list** in a moody, intimate space. [Banshee](https://www.bansheeatl.com/) is the more refined option on the EAV strip.

- Bartenders are knowledgeable. *Tell them what spirits you like and let them go off-menu.*
- Drink menu **changes with the seasons**
- Small plates pair well with drinks (don't skip the snack menu)
items.tip: Tell the bartender what spirits you like and let them make you something off-menu. They're good at it.

items.name: Crown and Conch
items.description:
A newer bar and restaurant in EAV with a **tropical-meets-Southern** vibe. *The patio is a nice hangout in warmer months.*

- **Rum cocktails** are strong and creative
- Seafood is surprisingly good for a bar
- The rum punch is dangerously easy to drink on a hot day
items.tip: Try the rum punch. It's dangerously easy to drink on a hot day.

lists.title: Top Things to Do on the Eastside Trail
lists.seoTitle: Top Things to Do on the Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail | atlanta news & talk
lists.seoDescription: The best activities, stops, and things to do along the Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail from Piedmont Park to Inman Park.
lists.intro:
The Eastside Trail is the most popular section of the Atlanta BeltLine, running about 2.25 miles from Piedmont Park down to Inman Park. It's the stretch that put the BeltLine on the map, and it's packed with things to see, eat, and do along the way.

You can walk the whole thing in about 45 minutes, but you'll want to budget way more time than that. There are too many good stops to just blow through it. Here are the best things to do along the Eastside Trail, roughly in order from north to south.

items.name: Piedmont Park Access Point
items.description:
Start your walk at the **southern end of [Piedmont Park](https://piedmontpark.org/)** where the trail connects. The transition from park to trail is seamless, and you get some of the best skyline views right at the beginning.

- Food vendors and buskers usually set up near the entry point
- *Start early on weekend mornings (before 9am) to beat the crowds*
- Great photo op with the Midtown skyline behind you
items.tip: Start early on weekend mornings (before 9am) to beat the crowds. The trail gets packed by mid-morning on nice days.

items.name: Ponce City Market
items.description:
The massive mixed-use development in the **old Sears building** is the biggest attraction on the trail. [Ponce City Market](https://poncecitymarket.com/) has something for everyone.

- **Central Food Hall** has dozens of vendors (skip the sit-down restaurants, graze the stalls instead)
- **The Roof** on top has carnival games and skyline views
- Shops are worth browsing, especially the local Atlanta brands
items.tip: The Central Food Hall on the main floor has the best food options. Skip the sit-down restaurants and graze the stalls instead.

items.name: Historic Fourth Ward Park
items.description:
This **award-winning park** sits right on the trail between Ponce City Market and the Inman Park section. *It's a perfect mid-walk breather.*

- **Splash pad** is perfect for kids in summer
- Walking loop around the stormwater pond is a nice detour from the main trail
- Bring a blanket and sit on the hill overlooking the pond
items.tip: Bring a blanket and sit on the hill overlooking the pond. It's one of the most relaxing spots on the whole BeltLine.

items.name: BeltLine Art Installations
items.description:
[Art on the Atlanta BeltLine](https://art.beltline.org/) is a curated public art program that commissions **temporary and permanent installations** along the trail. You'll see new stuff every few months.

- Murals, sculptures, and **interactive pieces** change regularly
- Download the BeltLine app to get info on current installations
- *Some of the interactive pieces are easy to walk right past, so keep your eyes open*
items.tip: Download the BeltLine app to get info on current installations. Some of the interactive pieces are easy to walk right past.

items.name: Krog Street Tunnel
items.description:
The **famous graffiti tunnel** that passes under railroad tracks near the southern end of the Eastside Trail. [Krog Street Tunnel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krog_Street_Tunnel) is a constantly evolving gallery of street art, tags, and murals. *Loud, colorful, and distinctly Atlanta.*

> The art changes weekly. If you see something you love, photograph it because it might be painted over by next weekend.
items.tip: The art changes weekly. If you see something you love, photograph it because it might be painted over by next week.

items.name: Krog Street Market
items.description:
A food hall in a **converted warehouse** at the southern end of the Eastside Trail. Smaller and more intimate than Ponce City Market. *The quality-to-crowd ratio is better here.*

- **Gu's Dumplings** (don't skip these)
- **Fred's Meat & Bread** for sandwiches
- **Jeni's Ice Creams** for dessert
- Back patio connects directly to the trail
items.tip: The back patio connects to the trail and is a great place to eat with a view of the BeltLine traffic.

items.name: New Realm Brewing
items.description:
A **massive brewery with a rooftop patio** directly on the BeltLine. [New Realm](https://www.newrealmbrewing.com/) has solid beers and a food menu that's more extensive than most breweries.

- **Rooftop views** of the Eastside Trail are great for watching the sunset
- The rooftop fills up fast on weekends, so *head up there first to grab a table*
- Good option for a mid-walk beer break
items.tip: The rooftop fills up fast on weekends. Head up there first to grab a table, then order at the bar.

items.name: Bike Rentals and Scooter Stations
items.description:
Multiple bike and scooter rental stations sit along the trail. **Riding the trail is a completely different experience than walking it.**

- [Relay Bike Share](https://relaybikeshare.com/atlanta/) has docking stations at several points
- Bird/Lime scooters are scattered throughout
- *Rent a bike over a scooter.* The trail has some narrow sections where scooters at full speed can be a hazard for pedestrians.
items.tip: Rent a bike over a scooter. The trail has some narrow sections where scooters at full speed can be a hazard for pedestrians.

items.name: Inman Park Neighborhood
items.description:
The southern terminus of the Eastside Trail puts you right in **[Inman Park](https://inmanpark.org/)**, one of Atlanta's most beautiful neighborhoods.

- Walk the **tree-lined streets** to see incredible Victorian architecture
- **Elizabeth Street** has some of the best Victorian homes in Atlanta
- The [Inman Park Festival](https://inmanparkfestival.com/) in April is one of the city's best events

*Even if you're just passing through, detour down a few side streets. It's worth it.*
items.tip: Walk down Elizabeth Street for some of the best Victorian homes in Atlanta. The butterfly sculpture at the corner is a popular photo spot.

items.name: Paris on Ponce
items.description:
A massive **vintage and antique store** on North Highland Avenue, a short walk from the trail. [Paris on Ponce](https://parisonponce.com/) is **50,000 square feet of curated chaos.**

- Old signs, furniture, art, oddities, and one-of-a-kind finds
- Even if you don't buy anything, it's worth the browse
- *Allow more time than you think. You can easily spend an hour wandering through the different rooms.*
items.tip: Allow more time than you think. You can easily spend an hour wandering through the different rooms and vendor booths.

lists.imageAlt: Sunset view of the Midtown Atlanta skyline from the Eastside BeltLine Trail
lists.imageCredit: Photo: Marc Merlin / Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA 4.0)

lists.title: Best Pizza in Atlanta ITP
lists.seoTitle: Best Pizza in Atlanta ITP | atlanta news & talk
lists.seoDescription: The best pizza spots inside the perimeter in Atlanta, from Neapolitan wood-fired to New York slices and Detroit-style. A local's pizza guide.
lists.intro:
Atlanta's pizza scene has come a long way. You can now get legitimate Neapolitan, proper New York-style slices, Detroit-style pan pizza, and creative pies that don't fit into any category. All inside the perimeter.

This list covers the pizza places that are actually worth driving across town for. Not chains, not franchises. These are independent spots run by people who care about dough, sauce, and cheese in that order.

items.name: Antico Pizza Napoletana
items.description:
**The place that put Atlanta on the pizza map.** Giovanni Di Palma's wood-fired Neapolitan pies are the real deal, made with imported Italian ingredients and cooked at 900 degrees.

- The **San Gennaro** (sausage, peppers, onions) is outstanding
- Communal seating and **BYOB policy** (no corkage fee) make it a great group spot

> Bring a bottle of wine and you've got a very affordable dinner at one of the best pizza places in the Southeast.
items.tip: It's BYOB with no corkage fee. Bring a bottle of wine and you've got a very affordable dinner at one of the best pizza places in the Southeast.

items.name: Ammazza
items.description:
Neapolitan-style pizza in Old Fourth Ward with a **72-hour fermented dough** and a massive wood-fired oven. *The margherita is a thing of beauty, simple and perfectly charred.*

- **Rooftop patio** overlooks the neighborhood
- No reservations, so put your name in on weekends and walk the BeltLine while you wait
- The dough is the star here
items.tip: No reservations. On weekends, put your name in and walk the BeltLine for 30 minutes while you wait.

items.name: O4W Pizza
items.description:
**New York-style slices** and whole pies in a casual counter-service spot. The slices are big, foldable, and properly greasy in the best way.

- **Pepperoni slice** is a go-to for a reason
- The **white pizza with ricotta** is an underrated pick
- They also do a mean calzone

*Two slices and a beer is the perfect quick lunch.*
items.tip: Grab two slices and a beer for a quick, satisfying lunch. The white pizza with ricotta is an underrated pick.

items.name: Slim & Husky's
items.description:
**Creative, thick-crust pizzas** with interesting topping combinations in a hip-hop-themed space. Started in Nashville and the Atlanta location delivers.

- Dough is made fresh daily
- Specialty pies like the **Cee-Lo Green** (with pesto) are worth trying
- *Build your own if the specialty pies feel too adventurous. The crust is the star, so keep toppings simple.*
items.tip: Build your own if the specialty pies feel too adventurous. The crust is the star here, so keep the toppings simple.

items.name: Junior's Pizza
items.description:
A **late-night staple** near the West End. New York-style slices that are perfect at 1am (and just as good at noon). Nothing fancy, just solid pizza at a fair price.

- The **cheese slice** is all you need
- Open late, making it your go-to **post-bar pizza destination**
- *The kind of slice shop every neighborhood needs.*
items.tip: Open late. This is your post-bar pizza destination. The cheese slice is all you need.

items.name: Varuni Napoli
items.description:
**Certified Neapolitan pizza** in Midtown using imported Italian ingredients and a custom-built oven. The dough recipe produces a beautifully blistered crust.

- **Margherita DOC** with buffalo mozzarella is the way to go
- Lunch special is an excellent value (personal pizza and a drink for well under $15)
- *The certification is real. This is as close to Naples as you'll get in Atlanta.*
items.tip: The lunch special is an excellent value. Get a personal pizza and a drink for well under $15.

items.name: Nina & Rafi
items.description:
A newer spot in Poncey-Highland doing **creative neo-Neapolitan pies**. The dough is excellent and the toppings are thoughtful. *A date-night pizza spot done right.*

- The **fungi pizza** with mixed mushrooms and truffle oil is worth the upcharge
- **Natural wine list** pairs perfectly with any pie
- Intimate space with a polished but relaxed feel
items.tip: The fungi pizza with mixed mushrooms and truffle oil is worth the upcharge. Pair it with something from the natural wine list.

items.name: Edgewood Pizza
items.description:
A **no-frills slice shop** on [Edgewood Avenue](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edgewood_Avenue) that's been feeding the late-night crowd for years. The slices are huge and the prices are right.

- Open **very late on weekends**
- The pepperoni slice at 2am hits different
- *Exactly the kind of pizza you want after a night out on Edgewood*
items.tip: Open very late on weekends. The pepperoni slice at 2am hits different.

items.name: Little Bear
items.description:
A Summerhill restaurant that does **excellent wood-fired pizzas** among other things. The menu is broader than just pizza, but the pies are consistently great.

- **Split a pizza and a few small plates** for the best experience
- The **burrata appetizer** pairs perfectly with any pie
- Cocktails are strong and the space is warm and inviting
items.tip: Split a pizza and a few small plates for the best experience. The burrata appetizer pairs perfectly with any pie.

items.name: Fred's Pizza
items.description:
A **Decatur institution** doing New York-style pizza since the '80s. The decor hasn't changed in decades, and the pizza is exactly as good as it's always been.

- **Cash only** (ATM next door)
- The **large cheese pizza** is the move for groups
- *A true original in a sea of newcomers. No frills, no gimmicks, just pizza.*
items.tip: Cash only. There's an ATM next door. The large cheese pizza is the move for groups.

lists.title: Dog-Friendly Spots in Grant Park
lists.seoTitle: Dog-Friendly Spots in Grant Park Atlanta | atlanta news & talk
lists.seoDescription: The best dog-friendly parks, patios, and spots in the Grant Park neighborhood of Atlanta. Where to take your pup in Grant Park.
lists.intro:
Grant Park is one of the most dog-friendly neighborhoods in Atlanta. Walk around on any given evening and you'll see dogs outnumbering people on some blocks. The neighborhood has great parks, dog-friendly patios, and a community that genuinely loves its four-legged residents.

If you live in Grant Park with a dog (or you're visiting with one), these are the places you need to know about.

items.name: Grant Park Dog Park (Hank Aaron Dog Park)
items.description:
The neighborhood's **dedicated off-leash dog park**, located in the southeast corner of [Grant Park](https://grantpark.org/) near Boulevard. Well-maintained with a strong regular crowd of friendly dog owners.

- **Separate areas** for large and small dogs
- Water fountains and **shade trees** throughout
- *Weekday mornings are the sweet spot: less crowded but still enough dogs for yours to socialize with.*
items.tip: Weekday mornings are the sweet spot. Less crowded than evenings but still enough dogs for yours to socialize with.

items.name: Grant Park Trails
items.description:
The park itself has multiple walking paths that loop through the green space. Dogs must be on-leash outside the dog park area, but the trails are **wide, shaded, and perfect for a long walk**.

- Full loop is about **1.5 miles**
- The path near the gazebo and around the perimeter is the quietest route
- *Avoid the [Zoo Atlanta](https://zooatlanta.org/) entrance area during peak hours if your dog gets overstimulated by crowds.*
items.tip: The path near the gazebo and around the perimeter is the quietest route. Avoid the Zoo Atlanta entrance area during peak hours.

items.name: Six Feet Under Pub & Fish House
items.description:
A rooftop seafood restaurant with one of the **best patios in Grant Park**. The rooftop is not dog-friendly, but the **ground-level patio welcomes dogs** (they'll bring a water bowl).

- **Fried fish tacos** are the standout
- Views of [Oakland Cemetery](https://oaklandcemetery.com/) make it a great stop
- *Fish and chips are the way to go if you're keeping it simple.*
items.tip: Request the ground-floor patio and they'll bring a water bowl for your dog. The fish and chips are the way to go.

items.name: Beacon Atlanta
items.description:
A **coffee shop and bar** on Memorial Drive with a spacious patio that's very dog-friendly. The outdoor seating area is big enough that dogs aren't bumping into other tables.

- **Good coffee** in the morning, **good beer** in the evening
- Patio has shade umbrellas and water bowls for dogs
- *A great work-from-patio spot on weekday mornings.*
items.tip: The patio has shade umbrellas and water bowls for dogs. It's a great work-from-patio spot on weekday mornings.

items.name: Dakota Blue
items.description:
A neighborhood bar and restaurant with a **casual patio that welcomes dogs**. The outdoor space is laid-back enough that your dog can relax under the table without stress.

- Menu is **comfort food done well**
- Weekend brunch is dog-patio friendly
- **Chicken and waffles** are solid
items.tip: The weekend brunch is dog-patio friendly. The chicken and waffles are solid.

items.name: Grant Park Farmers Market
items.description:
Held **every Sunday** at the park, this [farmers market](https://www.cfmatl.org/grant-park) is dog-friendly (on leash) and a great place to do a lap with your pup. *A neighborhood institution.*

- Local produce, baked goods, and coffee vendors
- Often has a **dog treat vendor**
- Get there between 9 and 10am for the best selection
items.tip: Get there between 9 and 10am for the best selection. Bring bags and keep your dog close in the crowded aisles.

items.name: Oakland Cemetery
items.description:
**Atlanta's oldest cemetery** is also a beautiful [48-acre greenspace](https://oaklandcemetery.com/). Dogs are allowed on-leash, and the wide paths, old trees, and historic monuments make it one of the most unique walks in the city.

- Peaceful and rarely crowded
- Walking paths have some of the **oldest trees** in the area

> The cemetery closes at dusk. Respect the grounds and clean up after your dog.
items.tip: The cemetery closes at dusk. Respect the grounds and clean up after your dog. The walking paths near the Confederate section have some of the oldest trees.

items.name: Eventide Brewing
items.description:
A Grant Park brewery with a **large outdoor area** and a very dog-friendly policy. Dogs are welcome in the outdoor space, and there's plenty of room for them to hang out while you enjoy a beer.

- Beer is solid and the vibe is casual
- They sometimes host **yappy hours** and dog-themed events
- *Check their [social media](https://www.instagram.com/eventidebrewing/) for upcoming ones.*
items.tip: They sometimes host yappy hours and dog-themed events. Check their social media for upcoming ones.

lists.imageAlt: Fountain at Grant Park in Atlanta
